content: Just as structured vocabulary reflection in high school English writing builds long-term retention and improves expressive accuracy, iterative self-reflexion for IT autonomous agents can be framed as a "vocabulary building" process for agent action spaces: agents curate and refine a "lexicon" of effective action patterns by reflecting on past outcomes, just as students refine a lexicon of effective linguistic phrasing through intentional reflection. This framing suggests that teaching agents reflexion could borrow spaced repetition techniques used for vocabulary learning to boost long-term retention of successful action schemas.
